name: title
description: Core Concepts

name: tagline
description: The authoritative vocabulary for the WBSKT workflow builder. Covers both the visual canvas UI and the underlying engine runtime — from the elements you drag and drop to the objects the engine creates and manages at runtime.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.2

name: badge
description: Unified Glossary
The visual objects and interactive surfaces that make up the workflow builder interface. Everything visible in the editor maps to a concept defined here.

name: Canvas
description: The infinite, pannable and zoomable workspace where a workflow is visually assembled by placing nodes and drawing connections between them.

name: Node
description: A single, self-contained processing unit placed on the canvas. Every action, decision, trigger, loop, and data operation in a workflow is represented as a node.

name: Trigger
type: Entry points that initiate workflow execution in response to an external event. No input ports.

name: Logic & Control
type: Conditional routing, branching, looping, and parallel join nodes. Shape the execution path.

name: Data
type: Read, transform, and write values in the WorkflowContext.

name: Action
type: Perform side-effects: send commands, call external APIs, dispatch notifications.

name: Connection · Wire
description: A directed line drawn from one node's output port to another node's input port. The route a Signal travels to activate the next node in the workflow.

name: Port
description: A typed attachment point rendered as a colored dot on a node's edge. Every port emits or receives a signal — the color indicates the kind of signal, not a different data carrier. Ports are the anchors for connections.

name: Properties Panel · Inspector
description: The configuration sidebar that opens when a node is selected on the canvas. Exposes all editable properties for that node type, organized by group.
The core objects the engine creates, manages, and executes. Understanding the distinction between a Definition and an Instance is central to working with WBSKT.

name: Workflow Definition
description: The versioned, saved blueprint that captures the complete canvas topology — every node, connection, port binding, and property value. The definition is the template; it is never itself "running".

name: Workflow Instance
description: A live, in-progress execution spawned from a Workflow Definition. Each instance is isolated — it has its own WorkflowContext, its own execution pointer, and its own lifecycle.

name: Trigger Node
description: A specialised class of node that listens to an external event source and initiates workflow execution. Trigger nodes have no input ports — they are always the starting point of a signal chain.

name: Signal
description: The zero-payload notification pulse that travels along a connection from one node's output port to the next node's input port. A signal carries no data — it is a pure "proceed" instruction.

name: WorkflowContext
description: The mutable, shared key-value store that lives inside a Workflow Instance. It is the sole data bus — all nodes read inputs from it and write outputs back to it rather than passing data through connections.
How the engine maps incoming trigger events to the correct workflow instance — or decides to create a new one.

name: CorrelationKey · Identity
description: The resolved string identifier extracted from an incoming trigger payload that determines which Workflow Instance should receive the event — or whether a new instance should be created.

name: MapOrCreate
type: If a matching instance is found, the signal is routed to it. If not, a new instance is created. Default.

name: MapOrDiscard
type: Only routes to an existing instance. If no match is found, the incoming event is silently dropped.

name: AlwaysCreate
type: Ignores the resolved key entirely and always spawns a new parallel instance. Useful for stateless, fire-and-forget event logs.

name: MapOrReplace
type: If a match is found, the existing instance is terminated and a fresh instance starts with the new payload. Use when the latest event must always win.

name: title
description: Set Context

name: tagline
description: The state manager. Explicitly creates, updates, or clears variables within the persistent WorkflowContext. Use this to normalize raw trigger data into clean, semantic variables for downstream logic.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: State Assignment

name: assignments
description: A list of operations to perform: { key, value, operation }. Key: The destination path (e.g., $.status). Value: Static data or expression (e.g., $.mqtt_1.payload.val * 1.1).

name: operation
description: Set — (Upsert) Creates a new key or overwrites an existing one. Merge — Shallow-merges an object into an existing key. Increment — Adds a value to a numeric variable. Append — Adds an item to an array. Clear — Removes the key from the context.

name: Implicit Initialization
description: If an Increment or Append operation is performed on a key that does not exist, the engine initializes it as 0 or [] respectively before applying the change.

name: Semantic Normalization
description: Raw payloads from triggers are often deeply nested (e.g., $.mqtt_1.body.sensors[0].val). Use a Set Context node at the start of your flow to map these to clean, reusable variables like $.currentTemperature. This makes your workflow modular and easier to maintain.

name: set
description: Triggers the assignment logic. All expressions are resolved against the current state before any writes occur.

name: onComplete
description: Fires once the new state has been committed to the persistent WorkflowInstance store.

name: onError
description: Fires if an expression fails (e.g., dividing by zero) or if an operation is invalid for the data type (e.g., incrementing a string).

name: title
description: Fan-In Gate

name: tagline
description: The synchronization barrier. Harvests multiple parallel trigger signals and releases a single unified pulse once its synchronization condition is met. Essential for re-joining concurrent loops and parallel branches.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: Sync Barrier

name: mode
description: All — Wait for every incoming signal to arrive before firing. Any — Fire as soon as the first signal arrives (Race mode). Quorum — Fire once a specific number of signals arrive.

name: expectedCount
description: The number of signals to wait for. auto uses the parent loop's iteration count.

name: waitTimeoutMs
description: Maximum time to wait for synchronization to complete. 0 = wait indefinitely.

name: collectResults
description: If true, the node "harvests" the shared state of all connected source nodes and consolidates them into a result array.

name: The Harvesting Model
description: In the WBSKT "Shared-State" model, Fan-In nodes do not "receive" data over wires. Instead, they perform a State Sweep of the WorkflowContext for all nodes connected to the arrive port, consolidating individual iteration results into a unified object.

name: arrive
description: Termination point for parallel branches. Each arrival increments the internal counter.

name: reset
description: Clears the current synchronization counter and harvested results.

name: onComplete
description: Fires when the sync condition (All/Any/Quorum) is met. Carries the harvested result array. | payload: { "summary": { "totalArrived": 10, "status": "Complete" }, "results": [ { "iteration": 0, "nodeId": "api_1", "data": { ... } }, ... ] }

name: onPartial
description: Fires if the workflow is interrupted or stopped before completion, providing a snapshot of current progress.

name: onTimeout
description: Fires if waitTimeoutMs is reached before the synchronization condition is met.

name: title
description: Logic Gate

name: tagline
description: The multi-rule decision engine. Evaluates one or more independent boolean expressions against the workflow context and routes the trigger pulse to specific output ports based on the result.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: Multi-Rule Router

name: logicMode
description: Exclusive — Evaluates top-to-bottom. Pulses the first matching rule and stops. Parallel — Evaluates all rules. Pulses every matching rule simultaneously.

name: rules
description: List of rules: { condition: expression, label: string }. The label becomes the output port name.

name: strictMode
description: true — Missing variables or null values trigger the onError port immediately (Recommended for safety-critical flows). false — Missing data resolves the specific condition to false instead of failing the node.

name: The Power of Parallel Evaluation
description: Unlike a standard If-Else, Parallel mode evaluates every rule in the list. This allows a single node to categorize a complex state and trigger multiple specific actions simultaneously. Example (Facility Safety): • Rule 1 (Fire): smoke > 0.5 → Port FireAlert • Rule 2 (Power): voltage < 110 → Port BackupGen If both conditions are met, both ports pulse. This is more efficient than chaining multiple If nodes and keeps the logic centralized.

name: eval
description: The trigger signal. When pulsed, the node evaluates the rules array based on the logicMode.

name: [ruleLabel]
description: A dynamic port created for each rule. Pulses if the rule's condition is true.

name: default
description: Fires only if no rules evaluate to true. Acts as the fallback or "else" path.

name: onError
description: Fires if an expression cannot be resolved due to syntax errors or missing data.

name: title
description: Switch Gate

name: tagline
description: The value-based router. Evaluates a single expression once and matches the result against multiple predefined cases, directing the trigger pulse to the matching output path.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: Value Matcher

name: expression
description: The value to evaluate (e.g., $.client.region or $.mqtt.payload.type).

name: matchMode
description: Exact — String or numeric equality. Regex — Matches the expression result against a regular expression pattern. NumericRange — Matches if the value falls within a range (e.g., 0-100).

name: cases
description: List of cases: { value: any, label: string }. The label becomes the output port name.

name: When to use Switch vs. Logic Gate
description: Use the Switch Gate when you are routing based on a single categorical variable (e.g., Device Type, Region, Status Code). Use the Logic Gate when you need to evaluate multiple independent conditions (e.g., temp > 50 AND humidity < 20).

name: eval
description: The trigger signal. When pulsed, the node resolves the expression and finds the matching case.

name: [caseLabel]
description: A dynamic port created for each case. Pulses if the expression result matches the case value.

name: default
description: Fires only if no cases match the expression result.

name: onError
description: Fires if the expression cannot be resolved or if the match mode fails due to invalid syntax.

name: title
description: Static For-Loop Node

name: tagline
description: A deterministic iteration node for bounded loops. Emits per-iteration output, supports pause/continue/stop control signals, and persists state for crash-safe resume behavior.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: Sequential by default

name: badge
description: IoT-aware ack model

name: badge
description: Dynamic loop — TODO

name: StaticForLoopNode
description: Iterates over a fixed numeric range defined at workflow design time. Range is set in the WorkflowDefinition and cannot be changed at runtime. On each iteration, the current value is emitted via the onIteration output port. Supports external pause, continue, and stop control via input signal ports.

name: from
description: Starting value of the loop (inclusive). e.g. from=0 starts the first iteration with value=0

name: to
description: Ending value of the loop (exclusive — like Python range()). e.g. from=0, to=5 yields values 0,1,2,3,4

name: step
description: Increment applied after each iteration. Use a negative value with from > to for countdown loops. e.g. from=10, to=0, step=-2 yields 10,8,6,4,2

name: iterationMode
description: Sequential — next iteration starts only after current completes (or is acked). Concurrent — all iterations fan-out as independent pointers simultaneously. Concurrent mode requires maxConcurrency to avoid flooding downstream systems.

name: iterationDelayMs
description: Milliseconds to wait after an iteration completes before starting the next. Only applies in Sequential mode.

name: ack
description: false — fire and forget. Loop advances immediately after onIteration. true — loop suspends and waits for a signal to the continue input port before advancing.

name: ackTimeoutMs
description: Milliseconds to wait for acknowledgement before timing out. 0 = wait indefinitely. Only relevant when ack: true. Timeout fires the onIterationTimeout port.

name: ackMode
description: Any — resume when any one connected downstream node sends ack. All — resume only when all connected downstream nodes have acked. Only relevant when ack: true.

name: maxConcurrency
description: Maximum concurrent iterations when iterationMode: Concurrent. Strongly recommended for IoT command sequences. Has no effect in Sequential mode.

name: onIterationError
description: Halt — stop the loop and fire onError. Continue — skip the failed iteration and advance. Retry(n) — retry the current iteration up to n times before applying Halt or Continue.

name: Design note — ack model applies to all loop types
description: The ack / ackTimeoutMs / ackMode properties and the pause/continue/stop input port pattern are intended to be consistent across all loop node variants.

name: title
description: Group / Folder Trigger

name: tagline
description: The dynamic fleet trigger. Binds a workflow to an entire hierarchy of devices (e.g., /Facility_A/Sensors/), automatically enrolling new clients as they are added to the platform registry.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: Identity: Group

name: groupPath
description: The registry path to watch (e.g., /Factory_1/HVAC/). Supports recursive matching.

name: includeSubgroups
description: If true, clients in deeper nested folders will also trigger this node.

name: correlationExpression
description: Required. Used to extract the specific Client ID from the incoming stream to map it to its own instance.

name: onMatch
description: MapOrCreate — (Genesis) Spawns a new instance for every new client detected in the folder. MapOrDiscard — (Signal) Only updates existing instances. Useful for bulk-monitoring specific active groups. AlwaysCreate — Not recommended (spawns unlinked parallel flows for every message). MapOrReplace — Resets the individual client's digital twin state on every signal.

name: Zero-Touch Scaling
description: Group triggers eliminate the need to update workflows when adding hardware. By organizing devices into folders (e.g., /Region/Site), your logic automatically scales to new sensors as soon as they are registered by the field technician.

name: onEvent
description: Fires when any client within the folder path publishes data. | payload: // Written to WorkflowContext[nodeId]: { "payload": { "vibration": 0.42 }, "client": { "id": "motor_22", "path": "/Factory/Line_1/Motors", "tags": [] } }

name: title
description: Policy-Based Trigger

name: tagline
description: The behavioral fleet trigger. Binds a workflow to specific registration policies or device profiles (e.g., Firmware_v2_Beta), allowing for automated fleet management and targeted CI/CD for hardware.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: Identity: Policy

name: policyName
description: The name of the registration policy to monitor.

name: triggerOnRegistration
description: If true, the workflow fires immediately when a device checks in for the first time using this policy.

name: correlationExpression
description: Required. Maps the signal to the correct client instance within the policy group.

name: onMatch
description: MapOrCreate — (Genesis) Standard fleet behavior. MapOrDiscard — (Signal) Only updates existing instances. AlwaysCreate — Not recommended (spawns unlinked parallel flows for every message). MapOrReplace — Frequently used for lifecycle flows (e.g., re-running a setup script on every check-in).

name: Smart Fleet Routing
description: Policy triggers allow for behavioral segregation. You can target all devices on a "Legacy" policy for a security audit workflow, while "Gold Tier" devices follow a high-priority automation path, regardless of their physical location.

name: onEvent
description: Fires when any client assigned to the policy publishes data. | payload: // Written to WorkflowContext[nodeId]: { "payload": { "fw_ver": "1.0.8" }, "client": { "id": "dev_404", "policy": "Legacy_Upgrade_Plan" } }

name: title
description: Single Client Trigger

name: tagline
description: The "Pinned" identity trigger. Binds a workflow to exactly one specific device in the registry, ensuring dedicated, isolated execution for high-value assets.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: Identity: Single

name: clientId
description: The unique identifier of the device in the platform registry.

name: filterExpression
description: Optional JSONPath to filter specific events from this client (e.g., $.type == 'ALARM').

name: correlationExpression
description: Implicit. Set to null as the clientId acts as the fixed correlation key for the instance.

name: onMatch
description: MapOrCreate — (Genesis) Creates the instance on first signal. Updates thereafter. MapOrDiscard — (Signal) Only accepts signals if the instance was manually started. AlwaysCreate — Not recommended for single client (spawns parallel flows for one device). MapOrReplace — Resets the device's digital twin state on every new signal.

name: Dedicated Workspaces
description: Single Client triggers are best for "Digital Twin" scenarios where a workflow represents the persistent state of one specific physical machine. No data parsing is needed to find the instance; the engine knows exactly which "room" to put the data in.

name: onEvent
description: Fires when the specific client publishes data or changes state. | payload: // Written to WorkflowContext[nodeId]: { "payload": { ... }, "client": { "id": "dev_01", "name": "Main Pump", "tags": ["critical"] } }

name: title
description: HTTP Webhook Trigger

name: tagline
description: The edge gateway for API-driven workflows. Exposes standard HTTP endpoints, authenticates incoming requests, extracts correlation identities, and releases normalized payloads into visual canvas nodes.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: RESTful Entry

name: path
description: The relative URL path (e.g., /v1/github/merge). Must be unique within the tenant.

name: method
description: POST | GET | PUT | DELETE

name: correlationExpression
description: A JSONPath or regex to extract a unique Instance Context ID (e.g., $.body.order_id).

name: onMatch
description: MapOrCreate — (Genesis) Creates a new instance if the resolved ID is unknown. Updates if known. MapOrDiscard — (Signal) Only updates existing instances. If no match is found, the signal is dropped. AlwaysCreate — Ignores the resolved ID and spawns a parallel instance (useful for stateless logs). MapOrReplace — Aborts the existing instance and starts a fresh one with the new payload.

name: authType
description: None — Public endpoint. ApiKey — Header validation (e.g., X-API-Key). BasicAuth — Native username and password decoding. HMAC — Cryptographic signature hashing (Best for Stripe/GitHub).

name: secretRef
description: Pointer to a securely stored secret vault reference used for validating incoming tokens.

name: responseMode
description: AckOnArrival — Responds 202 Accepted immediately. Does not wait for workflow execution. RespondOnComplete — Freezes HTTP connection and returns a snapshot of the WorkflowContext when execution halts.

name: successCode
description: Status code returned to the external platform upon successful webhook acceptance.

name: Shared State Architecture
description: Connections between nodes do not carry data payloads. Wires only transmit pure triggers (pulses). When a Webhook fires, it writes the payload directly into the WorkflowContext. Downstream nodes pull variables from this shared memory as needed.

name: onTrigger
description: Fires a control pulse to adjacent downstream nodes. No data is passed over the wire. | payload: // Committed directly to WorkflowContext[nodeId]: { "metadata": { "method": "POST", "timestamp": 1711395729 }, "headers": { "content-type": "application/json" }, "query": {}, "body": { ... } }

name: title
description: Manual / User Event Trigger

name: tagline
description: The human-in-the-loop entry point. Exposes a button or form within the dashboard or external portal to allow direct manual intervention in workflows.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: User-Driven

name: buttonLabel
description: The text displayed on the action button in the dashboard.

name: inputSchema
description: Defines form fields (text, select, toggle) to be filled by the user before execution.

name: requiredRoles
description: RBAC roles allowed to see and trigger this button.

name: correlationExpression
description: A JSONPath or static value to bind this manual action to a specific instance ID.

name: onMatch
description: MapOrCreate — (Genesis) Creates a new instance if the resolved ID is unknown. Updates if known. MapOrDiscard — (Signal) Only updates existing instances. If no match is found, the signal is dropped. AlwaysCreate — Ignores the resolved ID and spawns a parallel instance (useful for stateless logs). MapOrReplace — Aborts the existing instance and starts a fresh one with the new payload.

name: Hybrid Automation
description: Manual triggers are often used as "Overdrive" signals to force a workflow into a specific state or to provide missing data that automated triggers could not resolve.

name: onExecute
description: Fires immediately upon button click/form submission. | payload: // Written to WorkflowContext[nodeId]: { "user": { "id": "u_99", "email": "admin@wbskt.io" }, "input": { "reason": "Emergency Shutdown", "override_threshold": 95 } }

name: title
description: MQTT Telemetry Trigger

name: tagline
description: The persistent IoT backbone. Maintains long-lived subscriptions to message brokers, handles high-frequency telemetry, and decodes binary payloads for real-time workflow routing.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: IoT / Edge

name: brokerRef
description: Vault reference to broker details (host, port, TLS, credentials).

name: topic
description: The MQTT topic subscription string. Supports wildcards (e.g., telemetry/+/sensors).

name: qos
description: Quality of Service level for the subscription.

name: payloadFormat
description: JSON — Standard text parsing. Protobuf — Binary decoding via schema. RawBinary — Passes payload as hex/base64 string.

name: schemaRef
description: Reference to a .proto file or binary template in the WBSKT Registry. Required for Protobuf.

name: correlationExpression
description: JSONPath for payload fields, or $topic[index] for topic segments (e.g., $topic[1] for 2nd segment).

name: onMatch
description: MapOrCreate — (Genesis) Creates a new instance if the resolved ID is unknown. Updates if known. MapOrDiscard — (Signal) Only updates existing instances. If no match is found, the signal is dropped. AlwaysCreate — Ignores the resolved ID and spawns a parallel instance (useful for stateless logs). MapOrReplace — Aborts the existing instance and starts a fresh one with the new payload.

name: Topic-Based Routing
description: The MQTT Trigger is uniquely capable of extracting identity from the topic metadata. This allows the engine to route messages to the correct instance even if the payload is encrypted or in a proprietary binary format that cannot be parsed by the node itself.

name: onMessage
description: Fires instantly upon message arrival and successful decoding. | payload: // Written to WorkflowContext[nodeId]: { "metadata": { "topic": "v1/dev_99/status", "qos": 1, "retained": false }, "payload": { "temp": 24.5, "humidity": 48.2, "status": "ONLINE" } }

name: title
description: Polling / DB Watcher Trigger

name: tagline
description: The bridge for legacy systems. Actively monitors external databases, storage buckets, or spreadsheets for state changes and releases normalized data into the canvas.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: Pull-Driven

name: sourceType
description: SqlDatabase | S3Bucket | GoogleSheet | RestApi

name: connectionRef
description: Vault reference to credentials (e.g., connection string, AWS keys).

name: query
description: The SQL query, file path, or API endpoint to poll.

name: pollingInterval
description: Wait time between poll attempts.

name: pollingUnit
description: Seconds | Minutes | Hours

name: correlationExpression
description: A JSONPath or regex to extract a unique Instance Context ID from the result row.

name: onMatch
description: MapOrCreate — (Genesis) Creates a new instance if the resolved ID is unknown. Updates if known. MapOrDiscard — (Signal) Only updates existing instances. If no match is found, the signal is dropped. AlwaysCreate — Ignores the resolved ID and spawns a parallel instance (useful for stateless logs). MapOrReplace — Aborts the existing instance and starts a fresh one with the new payload.

name: deduplicationKey
description: Field used to ensure the same record isn't processed twice (e.g., $.id or $.last_modified).

name: Stateful Polling
description: To prevent re-triggering on the same data, the engine tracks the deduplicationKey in a high-speed cursor store. If a polled row matches a known key, it is skipped unless the data has changed.

name: onResult
description: Fires once for every record/row detected in the polling cycle. | payload: // Row data written to WorkflowContext[nodeId]: { "id": 1024, "status": "NEW", "created_at": "2026-03-26T10:00:00Z", "payload": { ... } }

name: title
description: Scheduled Trigger

name: tagline
description: The internal heartbeat of the engine. Automates workflow execution based on precise time intervals or complex calendar patterns. Unlike event triggers, it originates from the system clock.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.1

name: badge
description: Time-Driven

name: scheduleMode
description: Interval — Simple repetitive cadence (e.g. Every 5m). Cron — Advanced calendar logic using standard Crontab syntax.

name: intervalValue
description: The numeric value for interval mode. Minimum recommended: 1.

name: intervalUnit
description: Seconds | Minutes | Hours | Days

name: cronExpression
description: The Cron string (e.g. 0 8 * * 1-5 for 8AM Weekdays).

name: timeZone
description: Valid IANA timezone string. Critical for DST-aware scheduling.

name: correlationExpression
description: A JSONPath or regex to extract a unique Instance Context ID. Used for correlating schedule ticks to active instances.

name: onMatch
description: MapOrCreate — (Genesis) Creates a new instance if the resolved ID is unknown. Updates if known. MapOrDiscard — (Signal) Only updates existing instances. If no match is found, the signal is dropped. AlwaysCreate — Ignores the resolved ID and spawns a parallel instance (useful for stateless logs). MapOrReplace — Aborts the existing instance and starts a fresh one with the new payload.

name: misfirePolicy
description: FireImmediately — If the server was down during a tick, fire it as soon as it recovers. Discard — Ignore missed ticks and wait for the next scheduled occurrence.

name: Context Persistence
description: This node utilizes the Shared-State model. No data is passed via output connections. Upon triggering, the engine writes the triggeredAt timestamp and scheduleMeta directly to the WorkflowContext for downstream consumption.

name: onTick
description: Fires a control pulse to downstream nodes. No data payload is included in the transmission. | payload: // Written to WorkflowContext[nodeId]: { "timestamp": 1711395729, "iso": "2026-03-25T12:00:00Z", "mode": "Cron" }

name: title
description: Workflow Definitions

name: tagline
description: The structural blueprint defining execution physics, routing logic, and instance lifecycle. This configuration governs how the engine instantiates workflows across IoT, CI/CD, and Automation verticals.

name: badge
description: Spec v1.2

name: badge
description: Multi-Trigger Enabled

name: badge
description: State-Aware Persistence
Defines how a workflow is identified within the system and how incoming signals are mapped to specific execution instances.

name: definitionId
description: Immutable unique identifier for this workflow template.

name: name
description: The display name used within the WBSKT orchestration dashboard.

name: description
description: Internal documentation describing the workflow's business logic and expected triggers.

name: triggers
description: List of entry points (HTTP, MQTT, Cron). Supports "Logical OR" activation — any trigger can start the flow.

name: correlationExpression
description: A JSONPath/Regex to find the ID in this specific trigger. Example: Trigger A uses $.sensor_id, Trigger B uses $.body.mac_address.

name: onMatch
description: MapOrCreate — (Genesis) Creates a new instance if the resolved ID is unknown. Updates if known. MapOrDiscard — (Signal) Only updates existing instances. If no match is found, the signal is dropped. AlwaysCreate — Ignores the resolved ID and spawns a parallel instance (useful for stateless logs). MapOrReplace — Aborts the existing instance and starts a fresh one with the new payload.
Governs the lifecycle and performance characteristics of the workflow instance during its execution.

name: instanceMode
description: Persistent — Full DB state tracking (Safety/Business). Ephemeral — In-memory only (Speed/IoT High-Frequency).

name: executionTimeout
description: The absolute time limit for a workflow instance before forced termination.

name: maxActiveInstances
description: Concurrency cap to prevent system flooding or resource exhaustion.

name: priority
description: Execution queue priority. Higher numbers process first in congested environments.
Configuration for global fault tolerance and recovery mechanisms when nodes encounter failures.

name: globalErrorHandler
description: Fallback node triggered if any unhandled error occurs in the flow.

name: defaultRetryPolicy
description: Global maxAttempts and backoffStrategy for all failing nodes in this flow.

name: isRecoverable
description: If true, the engine attempts to resume the flow at the last checkpoint after a server restart.
Defines the security context, data validation rules, and audit log retention policies.

name: inputSchema
description: Validates trigger payload before instance creation. Rejects invalid data early.

name: secretRefs
description: Keys to fetch from secure Vault storage (API keys, DB credentials) at runtime.

name: retentionPeriod
description: How long to keep history of completed instances in the audit log.

name: Versioning Policy
description: By default, active instances stick to the version they were started with. Updates to the definition only apply to new instances created after publication.
